notes: Anonymous dimension block: vertical 5795-unit dimension with DIMDOT arrowheads on layer DIM; no wall geometry.

rationale.evidence: The definition name *D315 is an AutoCAD anonymous dimension block, and every visible entity sits on layer DIM with the three POINT entities on DEFPOINTS — the classic anatomy of an associative dimension's cached geometry. Two horizontal extension lines at y=33170.207 and y=27375.207 (handles 8C0E, 8C0F) with one vertical dimension line at x=41368.83 (8C10); the gap between extension lines is exactly 5795, matching the MTEXT. Two INSERTs of arrowhead block DIMDOT and the single MTEXT (handle 8C13, '5795') complete the vertical dimension structure.
rationale.rule: Anonymous *D### blocks whose contents are LINE + arrowhead-INSERT + MTEXT on a DIM layer plus DEFPOINTS points are dimension-geometry caches, not building geometry; the decisive confirmation is that the extension-line gap numerically equals the MTEXT value.
rationale.counterfactual: If the block contained polylines, arcs, or hatches on wall/architectural layers, or if the MTEXT value did not equal the extension-line separation, I would reconsider it as a partial plan view or symbol rather than a dimension cache.
rationale.uncertainty: The projection cannot show what physical feature this dimension measures or where its INSERT is placed in the host drawing, so whether the dimensioned object is a wall, opening, or something else is unknowable from the block contents alone.
